potential_missing.source_sentence: If they apply for a visa, they fill in the application and then make the payment.
Based on the document check, the visa is either approved or rejected, and then the status is updated.
If they view the status, the status is updated afterward.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: After logging in, they can apply for a visa, view the status, check the status, cancel the application, modify their details, view their details, or view the purpose of the application.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow a user to view the purpose of the application after they have logged in.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If they apply for a visa, they fill in the application and then make the payment. Once the payment is done, the application is accepted and its status is updated.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow a user to fill in the application after applying for a visa.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If they choose to view the purpose of the application, the documents are checked after that. Based on the document check, the visa is either approved or rejected, and then the status is updated.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow a user to view the status update based on the document check.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If they view the status, the status is updated afterward.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall automatically update the status after viewing it.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow a user to fill in the application after applying for a visa.
hallucinations.reason: There is no mention of filling in an application before making a payment in the source text.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow a user to view the status update based on the document check.
hallucinations.reason: The source text does not specify that viewing the purpose of the application leads to updating the status.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow a user to view their details after they have logged in.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall allow a user to modify their details after they have logged in.
duplicates.reason: Both requirements are redundant as modifying and viewing details can be considered the same action.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow a user to view the purpose of the application after they have logged in.
ambiguities.reason: It is unclear if 'viewing the purpose' is a separate step or part of viewing details.

prompt:
You are a strict requirements validator.
Compare rewritten requirements against the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "missing_requirements": [
    {
      "evidence_quote": "string",
      "suggested_requirement": "The system shall ..."
    }
  ],
  "hallucinations": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "duplicates": [
    {
      "requirement_a": "string",
      "requirement_b": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "ambiguities": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "pass|needs_review"
}

Source text:
<<<
A person can start by creating an account or by logging in.

If they create an account first, they can then log in.

After logging in, they can apply for a visa, view the status, check the status, cancel the application, modify their details, view their details, or view the purpose of the application.

If they apply for a visa, they fill in the application and then make the payment. Once the payment is done, the application is accepted and its status is updated.

If they choose to view the purpose of the application, the documents are checked after that. Based on the document check, the visa is either approved or rejected, and then the status is updated.

If they view the status, the status is updated afterward.

If they check the status, they can then give feedback.

If they cancel the application, modify their details, view their details, give feedback, or complete the status update, they then log out.

After logging out, it ends.
>>>

Rewritten requirements JSON:
{
  "rewritten_requirements": [
    "The system shall allow a user to create an account.",
    "The system shall allow a user to log in after they have created an account.",
    "The system shall allow a user to apply for a visa after they have logged in.",
    "The system shall allow a user to view the status after they have applied for a visa.",
    "The system shall allow a user to check the status after they have viewed it.",
    "The system shall allow a user to cancel an application after they have logged in.",
    "The system shall allow a user to modify their details after they have logged in.",
    "The system shall allow a user to view their details after they have logged in.",
    "The system shall allow a user to view the purpose of the application after they have logged in.",
    "The system shall allow a user to give feedback after they have checked the status.",
    "The system shall allow a user to log out after they have modified their details, viewed their details, given feedback, or completed a status update."
  ]
}
final_assessment_policy: moderate: needs_review if extraction/rewriting failed, no rewritten requirements were produced, exact duplicate rewritten requirements were detected, or the LLM validator reports 6 or more total issues across missing requirements, hallucinations, duplicates, and ambiguities
